company_profile.size_estimate: large, tens of thousands of employees globally
company_profile.business_model: Sells industrial automation and information hardware, software, and services to manufacturers.

signals.description: Public emphasis on expanding software and AI-enabled offerings within its industrial automation portfolio.

hypotheses.statement: The February 2026 emphasis on software and AI-enabled offerings suggests Rockwell is prioritizing modernization of its own product line, which may extend to advising customers on similar modernization.
Customers with a large existing installed hardware base may find it difficult to adopt newer AI-enabled software offerings without a modernization program.

evidence.evidence_text: Rockwell Automation is a global provider of industrial automation and digital transformation solutions, helping manufacturers connect people, processes, and technology to run smarter, safer, and more sustainable operations.

evidence.source: Rockwell Automation expands software and AI-enabled offerings (example fixture, not a live source)
evidence.evidence_text: Rockwell Automation expands software and AI-enabled offerings.

evidence.source: no specific source — general reasoning about installed-base modernization patterns
evidence.evidence_text: Large installed hardware bases commonly slow adoption of new software layers across the industrial automation sector.
evidence.staleness_justification: General industry reasoning, not tied to a specific dated event or announcement — staleness_days doesn't apply the way it does to a discrete signal.

reviewer_findings.reason: Rests on a single unsupported evidence item — acceptable to keep as a hypothesis awaiting validation, but not yet strong enough to promote to an inference.
